summary: Nafamarket Phase 2 end-to-end testing complete. Backend (19/19 pytest cases passing) covers: /auth/me new fields, referral +1 boost both parties, /reports CRUD, admin reports list & resolve, /reviews with rating_avg recompute, /listings/:id/use-boost with credit decrement, existing listings/payments/messages/admin. Frontend critical bug found and fixed: missing `referralCode` useState in Login.jsx caused a ReferenceError that blocked all login. After fix, end-to-end UI verified: Nafamarket branding (N logo + orange gradient + hero text), Login referral input visible, Profile shows verified badge, rating 5.0(1), NAFA-XXXXX code with Copier + Partager WhatsApp buttons and 2 boost credits, Admin tab 'Signalements (1)' lists reports with Résoudre + RESOLVED states, ListingDetail shows Contacter WhatsApp (primary), Message interne, Appeler, Partager, Signaler, verified seal next to seller, and Avis section.

ui_bugs.issue: `referralCode` state was used (lines 47, 125) but never declared with useState - ReferenceError crashed login flow entirely after sending OTP
ui_bugs.priority: CRITICAL (FIXED by testing agent: added `const [referralCode, setReferralCode] = useState('')`)

action_items: Review Login.jsx fix applied by testing agent (added missing useState for referralCode)

Consider also ensuring all useState declarations are linted/checked at build (eslint react/no-undef did not catch it because referralCode was referenced inside a JSX child but the missing import was a real ReferenceError at runtime)
critical_code_review_comments: Login.jsx: referralCode state was missing - illustrates need for ESLint no-undef pass on JSX
server.py is 800+ lines; consider splitting into routers (auth/listings/reviews/reports/admin) for maintainability
Admin payments revenue sum uses async generator inside sum() which is fine but could overflow with large datasets - consider an aggregation pipeline
/api/reviews unique index on (from_user_id, target_user_id) is correct but startup will fail on existing duplicate data; verify in production migration
Frontend bundle.js path /static/js/bundle.js indicates CRA - lint with react-hooks/exhaustive-deps recommended

success_rate.frontend: 100% after fix (all 6 phase-2 UI flows verified)

test_credentials: Admin phone=620000000 OTP=123456; any 9-digit phone with OTP 123456 for new users. Admin referral code observed: NAFA-1CCF1 (rotates per fresh seed)
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed listings, a TEST report (now resolved) and a TEST review during tests; left in DB for next agent
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_nafamarket.py - 19 cases pass in ~4s. Login.jsx had a critical missing useState that has been fixed. Test data is prefixed TEST_ for easy cleanup. Universal OTP=123456 works for any phone. Admin user is now 'Admin Nafamarket'.
rca of the issue: Login.jsx referenced `referralCode` and `setReferralCode` on lines 47 and 125-126 but `const [referralCode, setReferralCode] = useState('')` was never declared (only step/phone/otp/name/city/devOtp/loading were declared at lines 12-18). React threw 'ReferenceError: referralCode is not defined' as soon as the OTP step rendered the referral input field. Reproduction: navigate to /login, enter any phone, click 'Recevoir le code' -> red error overlay appears. Mitigation applied: inserted `const [referralCode, setReferralCode] = useState('');` between devOtp and loading state declarations in Login.jsx. Verified fix by completing admin login and reaching authenticated /profile and /admin pages.
